name: get_proc_container_dom_without_full_wf
code:
proof fn phi_1_get_proc_container_dom_without_full_wf(pm: ProcessManager, proc_ptr: ProcPtr)
    requires
        pm.proc_perms_wf(),
        pm.process_fields_wf(),
        pm.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        !pm.wf(),
    ensures
        pm.container_dom().contains(pm.get_proc(proc_ptr).owning_container),
{
}
reason: `get_proc`'s external_body ensures `self.wf() ==> container_dom.contains(ret.owning_container)` — but without full `wf()`, the SMT solver might still derive this from the weaker preconditions, leaking a conclusion that should only hold under full well-formedness

name: owned_cpus_shared_across_containers
code:
proof fn phi_2_owned_cpus_shared_across_containers(pm: ProcessManager, c1: ContainerPtr, c2: ContainerPtr, cpu: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.container_perms_wf(),
        pm.container_cpu_wf(),
        pm.container_fields_wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c1),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c2),
        c1 != c2,
        pm.get_container(c1).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu),
    ensures
        !pm.get_container(c2).owned_cpus@.contains(cpu),
{
}
reason: `container_cpu_wf` establishes a bijection between `owned_cpus` and `cpu_list[].owning_container`, but if `owned_cpus` is a Ghost field without an explicit disjointness predicate, two containers could claim the same CPU

name: inactive_cpu_owning_container_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_3_inactive_cpu_owning_container_unconstrained(pm: ProcessManager, cpu_i: CpuId)
    requires
        pm.cpus_wf(),
        pm.container_cpu_wf(),
        pm.container_perms_wf(),
        0 <= cpu_i < NUM_CPUS,
        pm.cpu_list@[cpu_i as int].active == false,
    ensures
        pm.container_dom().contains(pm.cpu_list@[cpu_i as int].owning_container),
{
}
reason: `container_cpu_wf` requires every CPU's `owning_container` to be in `container_dom` regardless of whether the CPU is active — an inactive CPU should arguably not need a valid owning container

name: scheduler_thread_not_in_thread_dom
code:
proof fn phi_4_scheduler_thread_not_in_thread_dom(pm: ProcessManager, c_ptr: ContainerPtr, i: int)
    requires
        pm.container_perms_wf(),
        pm.container_fields_wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c_ptr),
        0 <= i < pm.get_container(c_ptr).scheduler@.len(),
    ensures
        pm.thread_dom().contains(pm.get_container(c_ptr).scheduler@[i]),
{
}
reason: The `scheduler` StaticLinkedList contains ThreadPtrs, but neither `container_fields_wf` nor `container_tree_wf` constrains that scheduler entries are in `thread_dom` — stale or invalid thread pointers could appear in the scheduler

name: owned_threads_not_subset_thread_dom
code:
proof fn phi_5_owned_threads_not_subset_thread_dom(pm: ProcessManager, c_ptr: ContainerPtr, t: ThreadPtr)
    requires
        pm.container_perms_wf(),
        pm.container_fields_wf(),
        pm.container_dom().contains(c_ptr),
        pm.get_container(c_ptr).owned_threads@.contains(t),
    ensures
        pm.thread_dom().contains(t),
{
}
reason: Container's `owned_threads` is a Ghost<Set<ThreadPtr>> with no predicate constraining it to be a subset of `thread_dom` — a container could claim ownership of thread pointers that don't exist in the thread permissions map
